COMPANION TO HORN STUDIO
SILENCE PLEASE · NYC

3-D model — drag to rotate · scroll to zoom · drivers turn red when they collide · amber = inside build tolerance (tight)

Acoustic network — the numbers behind the picture

Profile — horizontal plane above axis · vertical plane below

Mouth view — flares, taps, drivers

Band map — where each section lives, and what limits it

Response preview — 1-D transmission-matrix, lossless

Shape-only preview: per-section levels auto-aligned (absolute SPL needs T/S → Hornresp), lossless network so notches are exaggerated — real chambers damp them; 1-D validity to ≈10 kHz. What it shows honestly: the true λ/4 notch (above the rule-of-thumb estimate — the expanding throat detunes it), the chamber low-pass, and branch absorption — each passive section's chamber shorts the horn at its resonance and eats the HF there (damp with stuffing).

Design checks — acoustics & physical fit

Hornresp hand-off (Multiple Entry Horn wizard / the Waslo calc sheet conventions)

Areas and volumes (S1–S4, Ap1, Vtc, Atc, Vrc) follow the the Waslo calc sheet convention: sums across all drivers of that section. Lengths (Lpt, Lrc) are per driver. In Hornresp use the Multiple Entry Horn wizard (Help → Input Wizard); conical segments Con12/Con23/Con34 join S1→S2→S3→S4. ME2 mapping (manual): select config ME1/ME2 via the Loudspeaker Configuration tool; each tap enters at a segment NODE with throat chamber Vtc/Atc and wall gate Ap1/Lp (throat-adapter mode — double-click the Ap1 label); sealed backs are Vrc/Lrc. Add the driver's cone front volume to Vtc yourself (Driver Front Volume tool — Hornresp does not add it). The Wizard is disabled in ME1/ME2 — use combined response.

Driver mounting & drilling schedule

Wood build — panels & compound miters (the Waslo calc sheet v5 method)

Design rules encoded here

MEH STUDIO · CC BY-NC 4.0 · Unity/MEH principles patented by T.J. — personal builds only, do not sell

Provenance

Strict rule: every check and constant in this tool traces to a cited source — US patents 6,411,718 & 8,284,976 · the Waslo calc sheet v5 · Keele 1975–83 · JW Sound Solana/SAWMOD/JMOD guides · B&C mechanical drawings · measured reference parts — or is explicitly labeled a solver allowance. Nothing is invented; conflicting sources are presented as tensions. Rear tubes on open-back mids are Waslo’s sealed mailing-tube canon (Ø 0.85·frame, length from Vrc); sealed-back drivers (5NSM38, 8NSM64) don’t need them. the Waslo calc sheet v5 (read directly from the sheet): mid port ≈ 2–2.5″ from the throat for a 1 kHz cutoff — “smaller is usually better, but hard to build”; canonical mids are four small 2″ open-backs on the widened #2 panels.

build 63 · slim reference slots along the horn · taps fill the corners · honest per-island placement menus · 10 true CAD bodies (18SW115 sub · 8MDN51 midrange added) · INSPECT engine in-tool (tap-scale invariants · fly-to-violation evidence · live panel) · 8 TRUE CAD bodies + datasheet presets (DCX464 on cd14 · 10HPL64 canon on pa3way · 12NDL76/12LW1400/18PZB100 · DE900TN/DE610/DE500, specs web-extracted from B&C/18Sound) · conformal pocket cups (sector-safe; orbit-reviewed) · chamfer taps on real 45° boards (on-surface law; floating-kidney class extinct) · pa3way reference chamfer mids (per-kind pockets) · intent-only saved state (program updates re-derive) · reference chamfer quadrant · printed mounts · sweep gate (no silent overlap/taps anywhere reachable) · driver-off-tap freedom · taps first-class (CR levers · shape canon · true-scale dims · n=2 seam-pair fix) · knuckle island LIVE (SAWMOD S2 passages + remote bandpass woofers, corpus-grounded) · drivers-first designer · numeric suite v2 (72 checks, blessed settled-states) · Hornresp ME2 mapping · B&C library from the mechanical drawings
